# Adham's Rocket Launch Tracker â€” llms.txt

> A single-file static web application that visualizes rocket launches and live satellite constellations on an interactive 3D globe. Designed as an educational tool for a middle school student.

## Project Overview

- **URL**: https://adham-rocket-launches.vercel.app
- **Repository**: https://github.com/sardoru/adham-rocket-launches
- **Type**: Static site (single HTML file, no build step, no backend)
- **Audience**: Middle school student interested in NASA and SpaceX

## File Structure

```
index.html     â€” Complete application: HTML structure, CSS styles, and JavaScript logic (~1,600 lines)
og-image.png   â€” Open Graph social preview image (1200Ă—630 PNG)
README.md      â€” Human-readable documentation
llms.txt       â€” This file (agent/LLM-readable project context)
```

## Technology Stack

All dependencies loaded via CDN. No package.json, no bundler, no build step.

- **Three.js v0.160.0** â€” 3D rendering engine. Used for particle systems (THREE.Points), orbital path lines (THREE.LineSegments), and BufferGeometry for efficient GPU rendering of 6,000+ satellites.
- **Globe.gl v2.35.1** â€” High-level 3D globe library built on three-globe and Three.js. Provides the Earth sphere, atmosphere, rings (launch sites), arcs (trajectories), and labels. Mounted on `#globe-container`.
- **satellite.js v5.0.0** â€” JavaScript implementation of SGP4/SDP4 orbital propagation. Converts Two-Line Element (TLE) data into real-time latitude/longitude/altitude positions.

## Data Sources (Free, No API Keys)

### Rocket Launches
- **API**: The Space Devs Launch Library 2
- **Endpoints**:
  - `https://ll.thespacedevs.com/2.2.0/launch/upcoming/?format=json&limit=80&mode=detailed`
  - `https://ll.thespacedevs.com/2.2.0/launch/previous/?format=json&limit=80&mode=detailed`
- **Fallback**: Hardcoded array of ~8 launches used when API is unreachable
- **Refresh**: Fetched once on page load

### Satellite TLE Data
- **API**: CelesTrak NORAD GP Element Sets
- **Endpoint pattern**: `https://celestrak.org/NORAD/elements/gp.php?GROUP={group}&FORMAT=tle`
- **Groups fetched**: `stations` (ISS), `starlink`, `oneweb`, `gps-ops`, `glo-ops`, `galileo`, `iridium-next`, `science`
- **Format**: Three-Line Element text (name + TLE line 1 + TLE line 2)
- **Parsing**: Custom `parseTLEText()` handles both 2-line and 3-line formats
- **Refresh**: Fetched once on page load; positions recomputed every 3 seconds via SGP4

## Application State

Key global variables in the `<script>` block:

| Variable | Type | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| `globe` | Globe.gl instance | The 3D globe object |
| `allLaunches` | `{ upcoming: [], past: [] }` | Parsed launch data |
| `satRawData` | `{ [key]: TLERecord[] }` | Raw TLE records per constellation |
| `satPointSystems` | `{ [key]: THREE.Points }` | Particle systems for satellite dots |
| `satPathLines` | `{ [key]: THREE.LineSegments }` | Line geometries for orbital paths |
| `SAT_CONSTELLATIONS` | Object | Config for each constellation (color, URL, enabled state) |

## Satellite Rendering Architecture

### Why Direct Three.js Instead of Globe.gl Layers

Globe.gl's built-in `pointsData` renders vertical columns (cylinders from surface to altitude), not floating dots. Its `customLayerData` requires creating individual Three.js objects per data point, which is too slow for 6,000+ satellites. Therefore, satellites are rendered by injecting Three.js objects directly into `globe.scene()`.

### Particle System (Dots)
- One `THREE.Points` per constellation added to `globe.scene()`
- Uses `THREE.BufferGeometry` with a `Float32Array` position attribute
- Updated every 3 seconds: SGP4 propagation â†’ lat/lng/alt â†’ 3D coordinates â†’ update buffer
- Coordinate conversion: `geoTo3D(lat, lng, alt)` converts geodetic to Cartesian (globe radius = 100)

### Orbital Paths (Lines)
- One `THREE.LineSegments` per constellation added to `globe.scene()`
- Each satellite's path = N time steps propagated via SGP4, connected as line segments
- Longitude-wrap detection: if distance between consecutive 3D points > 50 units, the segment is broken
- Updated every 30 seconds

### Altitude Scaling Formula
```
scaleAlt(altKm):
  raw = altKm / 6371
  if raw < 0.15: return raw + 0.02
  else: return 0.15 + sqrt(raw - 0.15) * 0.3
```
This compresses high-altitude orbits (GPS at 20,200 km) to stay visually close to the globe while keeping LEO (400 km) clearly above the surface.

## Constellation Configuration

| Key | Satellites | Altitude | Path Steps | Path Span | Default |
|-----|-----------|----------|------------|-----------|---------|
| `iss` | ~14 (stations) | 408 km | 200 | 92 min | ON |
| `starlink` | ~6,000 | 550 km | 40 | 46 min | ON |
| `gps` | ~32 | 20,200 km | 100 | 360 min | ON |
| `glonass` | ~24 | 19,100 km | 100 | 360 min | OFF |
| `galileo` | ~30 | 23,222 km | 100 | 360 min | OFF |
| `oneweb` | ~600 | 1,200 km | 40 | 46 min | OFF |
| `iridium` | ~66 | 780 km | 40 | 46 min | OFF |
| `science` | ~30 (sampled) | varies | 40 | 46 min | OFF |

## UI Components

### Header (fixed top)
- Logo with gradient text
- Stats: Total Launches, Upcoming count, Satellites Live count

### Satellite Panel (fixed top-left)
- Collapsible panel with toggle switches per constellation
- Shows satellite count per group
- Total tracked count at bottom
- Click any row to toggle constellation on/off (triggers `toggleConstellation(key)`)

### Sidebar (fixed right, 380px)
- Tabs: Upcoming / Past launches
- Search input (filters by name, rocket, provider, location)
- Filter buttons: All, SpaceX, NASA, ULA, Roscosmos
- Scrollable launch card list
- Cards show: provider, mission name, date, status badge, countdown timer

### Detail Panel (fixed bottom)
- Appears when a launch card is clicked
- Shows: rocket, date, pad, location, status, orbit, mission type
- Random fun fact from a pool of 20 space facts

### Globe Interactions
- Click launch card â†’ globe zooms to launch site, shows trajectory arc
- Auto-rotate at 0.4 speed
- Mouse/touch: rotate, zoom, pan

## Key Functions Reference

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `main()` | Entry point: init globe, fetch data, start updaters |
| `initGlobe()` | Configure globe.gl instance with all layers |
| `fetchLaunches()` | Fetch from Space Devs API, parse, fallback |
| `fetchConstellation(key)` | Fetch TLE data from CelesTrak, parse, store |
| `loadSatellites()` | Fetch all enabled constellations in parallel |
| `propagateSat(record, date)` | SGP4 propagation: TLE â†’ {lat, lng, alt} |
| `updateSatDots(key)` | Rebuild THREE.Points particle system for a constellation |
| `updateSatPaths(key)` | Rebuild THREE.LineSegments orbital paths for a constellation |
| `updateSatellitesOnGlobe()` | Update all constellation dots + stats |
| `updateOrbitalPaths()` | Update all constellation paths |
| `toggleConstellation(key)` | Toggle a constellation on/off, manage Three.js objects |
| `selectLaunch(id)` | Zoom globe to launch, show detail panel |
| `geoTo3D(lat, lng, alt)` | Convert geodetic coords to Three.js Cartesian (R=100) |
| `scaleAlt(altKm)` | Compress orbital altitude for visual display |

## Deployment

- **Host**: Vercel (auto-deploy on push to `main`)
- **Build**: None (static file served directly)
- **Domain**: `adham-rocket-launches.vercel.app`

## How to Modify

To add a new satellite constellation:
1. Add entry to `SAT_CONSTELLATIONS` with CelesTrak GROUP name, color, and icon
2. The fetch/render/toggle pipeline handles everything automatically

To change update intervals:
- Satellite dot positions: `setInterval` in `startSatUpdater()` (default 3000ms)
- Orbital path recomputation: `setInterval` in `startSatUpdater()` (default 30000ms)

To adjust visual appearance:
- Satellite dot sizes: `size` parameter in `updateSatDots()` THREE.PointsMaterial
- Path opacity: `opacity` parameter in `updateSatPaths()` THREE.LineBasicMaterial
- Altitude compression: modify `scaleAlt()` function
